title: Dev stacks as full-fidelity peers: local per-stack Directus + auto-snapshot + light seed. The dev-stack up bring-up gets the demo treatment by default — a per-stack Directus (via the new stack-snapshot/cmd/provision-plan runner that makes the M10 directus.ProvisionPlan/EnvContract contract executable) + a cache-first stacksnap replay of the public taxonomy+directus surfaces + the dev-min light seed (~1 org + ~10 users). Delivers corpus/ops/seeding-spec.md (dev-min preset + dev auto-seed) + corpus/ops/snapshot-spec.md (dev as a replay target + local Directus on dev).
kind: the meatiest v1.3 milestone — reuses M10's per-stack Directus + the M9/M7 snapshot/seed framework. New Go: a small CLI runner (stack-snapshot/cmd/provision-plan) making the library-only M10 contract executable. New bash: dev-stack/dev-setdress.sh + the dev-stack up set-dress wiring. New declarative data: stack-seeding/presets/dev-min.seed.yaml. No new platform code path.

tests.go_note: stack-snapshot 212 -> 223 (+11: the new cmd/provision-plan runner — 6 at build + 5 at harden); stack-seeding 232 -> 233 (+1: the dev-min preset pin extends presets_test.go to a 4-preset ordered set + floor test). alignment + clerkenstein unchanged. Go total 708 -> 720 (+12).

python_m13_touched.delta_vs_m12: 33 -> 38 (+5 at harden: DevStackSetdressWiring x3 — non-fatal failed set-dress, the re-run-hint flag-fidelity regression, --no-setdress skips; DevSetdress x2 — trailing-value-flag regression, value-flag happy path). (pytest collects 38 cases; 33 def test_ functions, the rest parametrized.)

tests.race: both Go modules pass -race -count=1 (12 stack-snapshot pkgs + 8 stack-seeding pkgs all ok)
tests.gofmt_vet: gofmt -l empty + go vet clean on both modules
tests.flake_gate: 5/5 clean — Go: stack-snapshot {cmd/provision-plan, directus} + stack-seeding {blueprint} 5x -race -shuffle=on; Python: dev-stack pytest 5x sequential (38 passed each run). 0 flakes.

coverage.note: harden ran 1 pass (recorded in progress.md). The M13 surface is 4 source files (one small Go runner + two bash CLIs + one declarative YAML).
coverage.cmd_provision_plan_main_go: 93.0% statements (90.7% -> 93.0% at harden); run() 100%, checkEnvContract 100%. Residual misses: the main() os.Exit shim + a printPlan defensive branch pinned-unreachable by TestDefaultEnvContract_AlwaysProdSafe_AcrossPorts (an invariant test).
coverage.blueprint_dev_min_loader: 100% (the dev-min preset parses + validates under strict KnownFields, pinned by presets_test.go).
coverage.bash_clis: no native bash coverage tool — exercised by subprocess integration tests (the project's documented CLI strategy); pytest 38 (one go-guarded real-build test pins build_cli).

delivered.dev_min_preset: stack-seeding/presets/dev-min.seed.yaml — ~1 org (Dev Sandbox) + 10 users (role mix ~1 admin/~6 members/~3 candidates, fixed admin dev@anthropos.test) + 1 month activity. Seeds <1s. Pinned by presets_test.go (4-preset ordered set dev-min < small-200 < mid-500 < large-1k + floor test). (M13-D1)
delivered.provision_plan_runner: stack-snapshot/cmd/provision-plan/main.go — makes the M10 directus.ProvisionPlan/EnvContract/Validate contract EXECUTABLE (was library-only). Prints the bootstrap->replay->boot recipe; --check-env firewall-validates a concrete per-stack Directus env against the prod-Directus rule (exit 1 on a prod-resolving env). One source of truth for the recipe + the firewall, reused by both kinds. (M13-D2)
delivered.dev_setdress: dev-stack/dev-setdress.sh — the set-dressing pass (default-on, non-fatal) run by dev-stack up: per-stack Directus recipe + firewall-check, cache-first stacksnap replay of taxonomy+directus into dev-N, then the dev-min seed. Escapes --no-snapshot (seed only) / --no-setdress (skip all). The set-dress's own n=0-dev guard (refuse N=0 without --force) is a SECOND layer above stackseed --reset's. (M13-D3)
delivered.dev_stack_wiring: dev-stack/dev-stack cmd_up — calls dev-setdress.sh after bring-up, default-on, non-fatal; the re-run hint echoes the SAME flags the bring-up used (no spurious --no-snapshot).
delivered.corpus_snapshot_spec: corpus/ops/snapshot-spec.md — new 'Dev as a full-fidelity peer (M13)' section (the 3-step pass + escapes + safety + prod-safety-holds) + dev added to the replay-CLI line + the stale 'cloud/S3 store = v1.3' claim fixed to v1.4.
delivered.corpus_seeding_spec: corpus/ops/seeding-spec.md — new 'The shipped presets' table (4 presets) + the 'dev-min — the dev auto-seed' subsection + the two-layer n=0-dev guard note + the stackseed dev-min CLI line.

review.scope: GREEN — all 5 sections checked; every overview Scope.In item maps to a delivered artifact; Out: items (generic skills -> M14 Fate-2, blob bytes -> v1.4 = DEF-M10-01) confirmed-covered; 0 TODO/FIXME/HACK in M13-touched source; M13-D1..D4 recorded; M13-Q1/Q2/Q3 resolved to D-numbers.
review.code_quality: GREEN — gofmt + go vet clean (both modules), shellcheck clean (both CLIs), py_compile clean; the runner is a pure recipe-printer + env-validator (no held resources), the bash CLIs build into a per-invocation mktemp BIN_DIR (no shared-dir race), the preset is declarative; no dead code, no cross-module reach-in; 0 must-fix.
review.docs: 1 fix (must-fix, FIXED) — the stack-snapshot README Packages table was missing the new cmd/provision-plan row (per-unit handbook-index contract); added it. The dev-stack README + both corpus delivery docs were already complete + accurate; all 4 cross-reference anchors resolve (GitHub slug rule); no numeric handbook test-count to reconcile.
review.tests: GREEN — full suites (not iter-subsets): stack-snapshot 223 + stack-seeding 233 Go funcs both -race -count=1 green; all Python suites green (dev-stack 38, stack-core+demo-stack 67, injection+verify 69); flake gate 5/5 (0 flakes); coverage pinned in harden (provision-plan 93%, directus + blueprint 100%).
review.decision_triage: M13-D1 (dev-min size) / M13-D2 (executable M10 contract) / M13-D3 (default-on-non-fatal) already blended into the corpus during build (verified accurate) — added (#M13-DN) reference tags at close; M13-D4 (build_cli cmd-pkg path bug) stays archive (maintainer-only implementation fix).
review.adversarial: 4 scenarios recorded (Phase 2c): replay-DSN offset-rewrite vs the firewall'd Directus env (replay's write-target is offset-derived via pg.DSNForOffset, never the base port); concurrent mktemp BIN_DIR (disjoint per invocation); --no-snapshot seed still guarded (stackseed's own isolation guard); N=0 recipe-printer boundary (printer is text-only, N=0 refusal is the caller's, pinned). 0 bugs surfaced; no documented-risk acceptance needed.
review.deferral_audit: GREEN — knowledge/plan/releases/01.30-stack-party/m13-dev-peers/audit-deferrals/deferral-audit-2026-06-07-m13-close.md — 1 inherited (DEF-M10-01 S3 blob bytes + cloud store -> v1.4, signed/unchanged), 0 repeat, 0 aged-out, 0 new; M13 added zero deferrals (all Fate-1).

completeness_ledger.done_fate1: all 5 sections (dev-min preset + its pin; the dev bring-up set-dressing pass = per-stack Directus via the provision-plan runner + cache-first snapshot replay + dev-min auto-seed; the n=0-dev guard preserved + doubled; the seeding-spec.md + snapshot-spec.md corpus deliveries + the README handbook-index fix).
completeness_ledger.confirmed_covered_fate2: the generic stack-* skill renames (incl. dev-up/dev-down) -> M14 (roadmap M14 In: list); the safety & security doc -> M15.
completeness_ledger.escape_hatch_release_scope_breaking: none (DEF-M10-01 S3 blob bytes + cloud store is INHERITED from v1.2, signed to v1.4 at v1.3 design — not introduced by M13).

notes: M13 is the meatiest v1.3 milestone — it converges dev with demo for DATA (M12 converged them for N-allocation). The key reuse insight (M13-D2): rather than re-encode the M10 Directus recipe in shell, a small Go runner (cmd/provision-plan) makes the library-only M10 contract executable, giving the recipe + the prod-Directus firewall ONE source of truth shared by both kinds. Prod-safety held unchanged: capture is never run on dev (replay only), media stays refs-only (blob bytes = v1.4), the read-side AssertPublicOnly + write-side isolation guard both hold, and the n=0-dev guard is now doubled (the set-dress pass + stackseed --reset). Harden fixed 3 robustness bugs inline (whitespace-env firewall fail-closed, the re-run-hint --no-snapshot leak, a set -u trailing-flag error), each mutation-pinned. Next: M14 (unified stack-* skills + dev-up/dev-down, hard-renamed).
